quizzes.lessonTitle: Verification, clearance and the defensible file
quizzes.title: Category 3 — Sewage & Black-Water Remediation — Knowledge Check

options: Clean water from a sanitary source such as a supply line
Grossly contaminated water containing harmful agents — e.g. sewage, flood water, rising ground water
Slightly used water with no health risk
Any water that is coloured or discoloured

questions.questionText: Category 1 or 2 water that is left and supports microbial growth or passes through contaminated materials:
options: Stays the same category permanently
Becomes safe to drink over time
Deteriorates to Category 3 and must be treated as such
Only changes category if a laboratory confirms it

questions.questionText: For a saturated Category 3 job, how should porous materials such as carpet, underlay and wet plasterboard generally be treated?
options: Dried in place and kept, because replacement is expensive
Discarded as contaminated waste — they usually cannot be effectively cleaned and verified
Sprayed with fragrance and left
Sent to general household recycling

questions.questionText: What must happen before any worker enters a Category 3 site?
options: Nothing — start extraction immediately to save time
A documented risk assessment and Safe Work Method Statement (SWMS), with vulnerable occupants removed and power isolated
Only a photo of the damage for the insurer
A coat of antimicrobial over everything

questions.questionText: Which is the minimum respiratory protection typically appropriate for hands-on sewage/black-water work, and what makes it effective?
options: A cloth mask, effective in all situations
A properly fit-tested P2 (or higher) respirator that seals against the face
No respirator is needed for water work
Any dust mask, worn loosely for comfort

questions.questionText: Where should contaminated Category 3 water generally be disposed of?
options: Into the nearest stormwater drain
Onto the garden or nature strip
To sewer under the water authority’s trade-waste rules, or via a licensed liquid-waste contractor
Left on site to evaporate

questions.questionText: When choosing an antimicrobial/disinfectant for a Category 3 job in Australia, you should:
options: Use any product with a US EPA registration as if it applied here
Use a product legal for the purpose in Australia (e.g. APVMA/TGA as applicable), following the Australian label and SDS
Mix bleach with an acid cleaner for extra strength
Apply antimicrobial over visible soiling without cleaning first

questions.questionText: Mould is found on a wall during a sewage remediation. What is the correct approach to airflow?
options: Aim air movers at the mould to dry it quickly
Contain, run AFDs and dehumidifiers, remediate the mould, and introduce air movers only after the area is cleaned and cleared
Increase fan speed to push spores outside
Ignore it and keep drying

questions.questionText: What is the correct order for cleaning and sanitising a salvageable surface?
options: Sanitise first, then remove soiling
Remove visible soiling first, then apply the antimicrobial and allow the required contact (dwell) time
Apply antimicrobial and wipe it straight off
Only sanitise; cleaning is unnecessary

questions.questionText: A Category 3 job is complete when:
options: The area looks tidy and smells clean
The contamination is removed, surfaces are cleaned and sanitised, materials are dried to the dry standard, any mould is cleared, and it is all documented
The water has been extracted
The customer says it looks fine
